title: Pittocaulon filare
taxonomy: Pittocaulon filare is a member of the flowering plant family Asteraceae. This plant is commonly known as the narrow-leaved geranium. The classification of Pittocaulon filare is as follows: it belongs to the genus Pittocaulon and the species P. filare. The genus Pittocaulon is a group of flowering plants native to South Africa. Pittocaulon filare is often used as an ornamental plant due to its attractive foliage and flowers.
uses: Pittocaulon filare, a species of plant in the daisy family, has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various ailments.
